label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which of the options is NOT a reason why the English language spread to numerous parts of the world. Looking back at the provided text, it says: "The English language was first introduced to the Americas by British colonization, beginning in 1607 in Jamestown, Virginia. Similarly, the language spread to numerous other parts of the world as a result of British trade and colonization elsewhere and the spread of the former British Empire, which, by 1921, held sway over a population of 470-570 million people..." So the reasons given are British colonization and trade, as well as the spread of the former British Empire (which is the same as British colonization, perhaps). The options are: (A) British colonization (B) the spread of the former English Empire (C) British trade around the world (D) a large British population The text mentions British colonization, British trade, and the spread of the British Empire as reasons. It doesn't mention a large British population as a reason for the spread. The population figure given is about how many people were under British rule, not the size
